dinner.why_ordered: i had a nap once i arrived at my boyfriend’s accommodation, as he was ill we decided to order nandos for dinner

lunch.source: leftovers from yesterday’s food delivery + cooked at home

days.daily_reflection: So I don't usually eat breakfast, which is why I skipped it today. I woke up, around a normal time, like, somewhere between nine and ten. And I usually don't feel that hungry. I do usually have, like, a cup of tea and just leave it at that. So yesterday, because I ordered Nando's, I still had some spicy rice left over and some of my, mum's, like, pre made food that I just heat in the oven. So I had her lamb and spicy rice for lunch, and then I really wanted to make lasagna for dinner. So I popped over to Lidl, bought all the ingredients, and then came back and made lasagna to eat for dinner. This usually does follow my day to day. I don't usually feel that hungry in the morning, and I tend to eat, either lunch or dinner as like a lighter meal than the other. It just depends on how hungry I am, what time it is and yeah because my lunch and dinner times usually vary depending on what I do in the day.

So due to having an event the day before, I ended up staying awake until seven AM on that day, so I didn't have any breakfast. I got in the car. It's a four hour car journey. I fell asleep the entire journey. I got home around here at twelve PM ish and fell asleep again until five PM. So I don't have breakfast and I don't have lunch, which is why both these meals are skipped. I also don't feel hungry, so it personally, it didn't make a difference to me. I also had another party the same day. So once I woke up, I had a few brioche buns. And I wouldn't call that a meal. And then I went to the party, and I got there around nine. And I stayed there again until six AM, so I didn't eat any proper meals during the day. That doesn't usually reflect on my day to day eating skills. I habits even. I usually at least eat one meal. I breakfast is usually something I skipped or I usually have something that combines lunch and dinner, but this was more just snacks than it was very basically brioche ones, but that's it.

dinner.why_ordered: me and my boyfriend came home at 6am that day and slept until 5pm, once we cleaned up we didnt feel like making late lunch/early dinner and decided to order instead as we were craving food from our cuisine (south asian). we chose deliveroo due to the free delivery and because we saw a restaurant on here as an option a while ago and we remembered it was on deliveroo

days.daily_reflection: So today, I traveled home, which is why I didn't eat any breakfast. I usually just have a tea in the morning. And, again, breakfast is not usually something I eat anyway. And, for lunch, my mom made food, which was string hoppers, mutton curry, and I had a mutton roll as well. And, yeah, I just had some business to do at home. So I came back to uni, and I didn't eat dinner. I just had some snacks. And I've just been a bit also I didn't feel like cooking anything. Didn't really feel like eating, which is today's food. Usually, again, I usually do skip breakfast, but it's usually lunch or dinner will be something more fitting or yeah. One of them will end up being skipped. I do try to have something for dinner, but I just don't feel like it today. So it's not usually like my day today, but can be, like, can be submitted to one of my day to days.
